title: Expedition staging surfaces
description: Add deliberate tool, rope, light, and container staging using ordinary parser inventory.

title: Trap-door and route-state observation
description: Expose canonical access, light, and route conditions without bypassing movement.

title: Sounds, drafts, damp, and warning evidence
description: Communicate nearby underground conditions through bounded sensory clues.

title: Carried-hazard screening
description: Recognize smoke, heat, water, cursed, living, or unstable objects entering home.

title: Recoverable containment choices
description: Support limited barriers, storage, and quarantine with real house objects.

title: Home-intrusion event hooks
description: Define deterministic hooks for thieves, creatures, water, smoke, and supernatural effects.

title: Cellar threshold qualification
description: Prove staging, route authority, warning state, containment, hooks, and restore.

title: Cellar threshold capstone
description: Publish stable hooks for vulnerability, visitors, and the archive.

title: Deterministic correspondence queue
description: Schedule authored notes and deliveries from meaningful world events.

title: Letter and note provenance
description: Record sender, trigger, location, authenticity, annotations, and archive links.

title: Mailbox delivery and retrieval
description: Preserve the leaflet and canonical mailbox while adding ordered, nonduplicating mail.

title: Bounded replies and outgoing mail
description: Permit selected authored responses, not free-form correspondence.

title: Knocks, visitors, and optional entry
description: Handle a small set of stateful visits with refusal, absence, and return behavior.

title: Missed-delivery and revisit behavior
description: Persist unopened mail, notices, returned items, and visitor evidence.

title: Correspondence gameplay and persistence
description: Prove ordering, provenance, duplicates, replies, visits, absence, and restore.

title: Correspondence and visitors capstone
description: Document the message contract and provide clean inputs for the Attic.

title: Canonical archive-record schema
description: Represent people, places, objects, messages, incidents, quotes, outcomes, and links.

title: Late-1970s physical media taxonomy
description: Map records onto folders, cards, printouts, cassettes, film, microfiche, photos, and boxes.

title: Card catalog and deterministic indexing
description: Index by person, place, object, incident, chronology, and expedition.

title: Attic filing surfaces and capacity model
description: Create cabinets, drawers, boxes, shelves, recorder, projector, and terminal interfaces.

title: Archive query and retrieval commands
description: Support READ, FIND, FILE, PLAY, REVIEW, SHOW, and CROSS-REFERENCE explicitly.

title: Provenance, truth status, and annotations
description: Track source, confidence, contradictions, verification, redaction, and missing evidence.

title: Archive save/restore and migration
description: Persist records and indices, reject duplicates, and define schema migrations.

title: Attic archive-core capstone
description: Qualify retrieval and publish stable APIs for dossiers, cases, and playback.
